Home / Services / Lawn Aeration

Lawn Aeration

Unlock a healthier, greener lawn with our professional aeration services.

Is your Boise lawn struggling to thrive? Compacted soil can prevent essential nutrients, water, and air from reaching the roots. Our core aeration service uses specialized equipment to remove small plugs of soil, relieving compaction and allowing your lawn to breathe again. This process stimulates root growth, improves water infiltration, and reduces thatch buildup, leading to a healthier, more vibrant lawn that can withstand Boise's hot summers and cold winters. We recommend aeration in the spring or fall for optimal results, especially for lawns with heavy clay soil common in some areas of Boise.

Our team will carefully assess your lawn's condition and determine the best aeration strategy. We'll mark any sprinkler heads or underground utilities to avoid damage during the process. After aeration, we'll lightly fertilize your lawn to promote rapid recovery and growth. Don't let compacted soil hold your lawn back – invest in aeration and see the difference it can make.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Lawn Assessment - We evaluate your lawn's condition and identify areas of compaction.
  2. 02 Step 2: Marking Utilities - We mark sprinkler heads and underground utilities to prevent damage.
  3. 03 Step 3: Core Aeration - We use specialized equipment to remove soil plugs.
  4. 04 Step 4: Fertilization - We apply a light fertilizer to promote recovery and growth.

Benefits

  • Improved root growth
  • Increased water infiltration
  • Reduced thatch buildup
  • Healthier, greener lawn

Frequently Asked Questions

When is the best time to aerate my Boise lawn?

Spring or fall are the ideal times for aeration in Boise, as the soil is typically moist and the grass is actively growing.

How often should I aerate my lawn?

We recommend aerating your lawn every 1-2 years, depending on soil compaction and lawn usage.

Will aeration damage my sprinkler system?

We take precautions to mark and avoid sprinkler heads during aeration, minimizing the risk of damage.
